note: Initialized VibeGeometry with Epiphany-style persistence surfaces adapted to Blender 5.0+ geometry-node scene generation.

Geometry Script add-on is installed under Blender 5.1 and imports headlessly. A trivial cube node group built successfully in Blender 5.1.1; the documented grid().mesh_to_points() chain failed with NoneType callable, so Geometry Script should be treated as promising but each idiom must be smoke-tested against the installed Blender/add-on pair.

Local Geometry Script 5.1 generated docs are the authoritative node-signature source for this workspace. The prior grid().mesh_to_points() probe failed because grid() returns a result object with mesh and uv_map; the valid chain is grid(...).mesh.mesh_to_points(...).

Public Geometry Nodes learning material is mostly screenshots, videos, and downloadable .blend files. VibeGeometry should build durable Geometry Script knowledge by inspecting source .blend node groups, mapping one graph at a time in prose, then recreating and smoke-testing with local Geometry Script 5.1 signatures.

Completed first Geometry Nodes translation corpus entry: inspected Shriinivas/cartesian.blend NodeGroup.001, mapped its curve/vector field flow, recreated it as examples/geometry_script/shriinivas_cartesian_helper.py, and smoke-tested generation/save/reinspection in Blender 5.1.1. Generated node groups need fake users or real users before saving.

Verified translated Shriinivas cartesian helper behavior against source NodeGroup.001 by wrapping both helpers as Curve+Vector -> Set Position -> Curve to Mesh and comparing evaluated mesh vertices. Test used Count=25, Start=(-0.45,0,0), End=(0.45,0,0), r=0.6, Value=1.25; source and translation both produced 200 vertices with max delta 0.0.

Translated and verified all four inspected Shriinivas/cartesian.blend graph groups: helper, line, parabola, and circle. Verification compares evaluated meshes in Blender 5.1.1; helper/line/parabola match direct vertex order with max delta 0.0, circle matches sorted vertex set with max sorted delta 0.0.

Reverted direct AppData Geometry Script surgery. Cloned upstream Geometry Script into ignored external/geometry-script, committed local branch vibegeometry/blender-5-nested-tree-groups with nested @tree group fix, stored patch under patches/geometry-script, and added setup_geometry_script_clone.ps1 plus loader so VibeGeometry uses the repo-local patched clone.

Translated and verified Shriinivas/piechart.blend small graph family: NodeGroup -> VG Pie Segment and Pie Chart -> VG Pie Chart. Segment and chart evaluated mesh comparisons both passed in Blender 5.1.1 with sorted max vertex delta 0.0. Doctrine added: reusable groups can pass invisible scalar state such as End angle alongside visible geometry.

Translated and verified Shriinivas/fieldvalue.blend Digit At scalar helper as VG Digit At. Verification wraps source and translation by driving Mesh Line.Count and matches four number/position cases, including zero output. Doctrine added: value-only node groups need geometry-producing verification harnesses.

Extended fieldvalue translation with Create Decimal -> VG Create Decimal. Verified against source by comparing evaluated dot geometry: both source and translation produce 32 vertices with sorted max vertex delta 0.0. Doctrine added: tiny glyph marks such as decimal points should be isolated as named reusable parts when they carry stable display intent.

Extended fieldvalue translation with Create Segment -> VG Create Segment. Verified horizontal and vertical segment cases against source with 6 vertices each and sorted max vertex delta 0.0. Doctrine added: field masks can act as chisels over regular seed meshes, gating vertex offsets by local position conditions.

Created and pushed GameCult/VibeGeometry remote. Forked Geometry Script to GameCult/geometry-script, pushed branch vibegeometry/blender-5-nested-tree-groups, and opened upstream PR https://github.com/carson-katri/geometry-script/pull/69 for the nested @tree group reference fix.

Pruned inherited upstream-only branches gh-pages and nodes_to_script from GameCult/geometry-script. The fork branch surface is intentionally main plus vibegeometry/blender-5-nested-tree-groups while upstream may retain its own historical branch soup.

Upstream Geometry Script nodes_to_script branch is a real node-tree-to-script prototype from 2023-03-01. It adds operators/convert_tree.py, which walks a node tree and emits a @tree script text datablock, but it predates Blender 4 interface migration and later main-branch fixes, so VibeGeometry should treat it as a translation assistant candidate to port/test rather than a current drop-in dependency.

Extended fieldvalue translation with Seven Segments -> VG Seven Segments. Verified against source with 74 vertices and sorted max vertex delta 0.0 in Blender 5.1.1. Doctrine added: chassis groups compose tested parts into a joined assembly while preserving part intent in script.

Extended fieldvalue translation with Delete Segments -> VG Delete Segments. Verified source and translation across 195 digit/segment-position cases with zero mismatches by driving the boolean output into a Mesh Line count harness. Doctrine added: dense boolean node regions often encode lookup tables and should be translated into named clauses plus full-grid verification.

Extended fieldvalue translation with Next Digit -> VG Next Digit. Verified four branch cases spanning both sides of Max Precision by driving Result into Mesh Line.Count; all source and translated counts matched.

Full fieldvalue Field Value graph is a 176-node composition with menu switches, capture attributes across domains, sample-index reads, instancing, transforms, delete geometry, and material assignment. Manual one-pass translation would be high-risk; next action should test or port upstream Geometry Script nodes_to_script as a generated first-draft tool before attempting the full graph.

Reframed procedural doctrine as a spatial reasoning playbook rather than abstraction glossary. Each doctrine pattern should describe the visual cue, mental move, Geometry Script code shape, and verification cue so metaphors help an agent choose tools while reasoning about form.

Audited procedural doctrine snippets against the verified Geometry Script translations and removed conceptual shorthand. Doctrine snippets should use the same explicit Geometry Script node-call style as the scripts that passed Blender verification, with excerpts clearly behaving as excerpts rather than pseudocode.

Ported upstream Geometry Script nodes_to_script prototype onto GameCult fork branch vibegeometry/blender-5-nodes-to-script. Added callable convert_node_tree(tree), UI operator registration, patch persistence, and a Blender smoke harness. In Blender 5.1.1 the converter emits compiling draft scripts for Create Decimal, Digit At, Next Digit, Seven Segments, Delete Segments, and the 176-node Field Value group; generated output is a bootstrap draft requiring cleanup and behavioral verification.

nodes_to_script output is evidence and scaffolding, not style guidance. Use it to recover wiring/defaults/socket order and hidden graph structure, then rewrite into doctrine-quality Geometry Script with spatial names, intentional decomposition, and behavioral verification.

Updated VibeGeometry priors: Python is the authoring and orchestration layer, Geometry Script is the inspectable node-graph emission surface, bpy handles Blender scene/render/evaluation machinery, and external Python tools handle inspection, converter drafts, verification, docs, and corpus evidence. Do not reason as if the Geometry Script DSL is the whole toolchain.

Completed and verified full VG Field Value translation, including Blender 5 capture-item helper and Geometry Script menu-socket default tolerance patch. Added and verified paramnpolareq translations for equation helpers, Archimedes spiral, epicycloid, and mirrored root spiral.
Started CurveToMeshUV utility sample by translating and verifying Auto Smooth; main Curve to Mesh UV remains a staged utility-quarry target rather than a one-pass translation.

Finished remaining local sample graph families: extended pie clones/compositor, param/polar selector and wrapper, and full CurveToMeshUV utility/demo/title with mesh, UV-field, and caps-mask harness verification.

Expanded the verified Geometry Nodes translation corpus with official Blender demo samples: instance attributes grass tuft, index-of-nearest helpers, shortest-path labyrinth, and raycast minigame helpers. All new verifiers pass in Blender 5.1.1 with sorted max vertex delta 0.0 across their harness cases. Deferred official samples are classified as legacy-node, gizmo-node, simulation-zone, or repeat-zone targets rather than padded into doctrine.

Started IRCSS French Houses city-scale corpus target. Inspected 108 geometry node groups and verified first foothold MakeSpiral -> VG Make Spiral with 200 ordered curve points matching source at max delta 0.0. Recorded blockers from larger city graphs: repeat-zone feedback state, repeat Top output sockets, foreach zones, gizmo nodes, converter duplicate-key failures, and empty-output harness rejection.

Distilled persistent state: handoff now points to corpus docs instead of duplicating completed graph lists; implementation plan reflects post-corpus contract and IRCSS frontier; compaction helper warns on bloated handoff and stale first-corpus planning.

Added IRCSS PointyGothicCone translation as VG Pointy Gothic Cone; plain and ornamented harness cases match source evaluated geometry with sorted max vertex delta 0.0, proving ornament can be grown from captured structural ribs after instancing.

Patched Geometry Script nodes_to_script on branch vibegeometry/blender-5-nodes-to-script to drop duplicate property keywords when a same-named input socket exists; GenerateArc and MakeStairs now compile from converter drafts, and expanded IRCSS smoke passes for 11 target groups.

Mapped IRCSS French Houses at file level: 108 node groups, 63 modifier roots, 163 dependency edges, composer/organ/utility hierarchy, dependency hubs, blocker feature counts, and next target candidates. Inspector now records nested GeometryNodeGroup node_tree references.

IRCSS final result is object-level scene composition, not a single master town graph: visible collections combine generated houses, church/cathedral parts, walls/gate/bridge, farms, terrain, river, and set-dressing roots; top evaluated roots are HousesOnCurves and Geometry Nodes.002 cathedral.

Accounted for all 108 IRCSS French Houses geometry node group datablocks in a structural node atlas, including modifier use, dependencies, feature pressure, asset bindings, and translation posture. This upgrades corpus knowledge from scene-level map to per-graph coverage, while preserving the boundary that only accepted translations have behavioral equivalence proof.

Studied IRCSS French Houses below the group level: 16,889 inspected nodes, 138 unique node types, 844 artist frame labels, 273 nested group nodes, and complete mechanism tables for all 108 groups. Distilled the file into reusable translation doctrine: edge-first architecture, curves as attachment rails, capture before domain changes, Python tables for orchestration, and repeat/foreach/gizmo/closure as toolchain grammar rather than incidental noise.

Built a from-scratch Aetheria Bloom habitat study after reading Bloom lore and Bruneton Rama making-of references. Refocused from Kappa detail to whole-cylinder spaces: layered shell, axial hub/light spine, spokes, central water band, plains/farms, forests, city clusters, wrapped roads/rivers, cloud patches, utility routes, and one small service-ring marker. Blender verification passes with VG Bloom Light Spine at 21 nodes/31 links and 98 geometry/text objects.

Reworked the Aetheria Bloom build from a cutout/map-layer view into a full 360-degree whole-habitat composition: full shell layers, despun center-axis core and docking hub cap, spun spire sheath, multi-station major spokes, thin utility spokes, frame-transfer arteries, hub-cap terrace slums, favela/hyperurban belts, luxury spoke districts, mixed urban/suburban/industrial belts, industrial/agricultural areas, beach and sea bands, roads/rivers, and airflow cloud patches. Verification now passes with 481 geometry/text objects and VG Bloom Light Spine still 21 nodes/31 links.

Aetheria Bloom whole-habitat pass now uses shared cylindrical coordinate helpers, golden-angle spiral spoke/transfer loops, and explicit layered endcaps; Blender verifier passed with 735 geometry/text objects and light-spine Geometry Script group at 21 nodes/31 links.

Bloom detail pass added batched fractal favela wall detail, elaborated spoke plazas, and nested town fields; initial per-object version timed out, batching preserved visual detail and verifier passed with 1102 geometry/text objects.

Corrected Bloom terrace-slum orientation against lore: on hubward endcaps, up is inward toward the Spire, so slums are annular shelves around the docking hub office complex growing outward to the surface; removed the misleading cylinder-wall favela framing and verifier now checks hubward_endcap_rice_paddy_slums objects.

Bloom endcap terrace slums now use fractal value-noise occupancy rather than neat concentric box rings; verified dedicated hubward-endcap render and mesh-density checks for poor shacks, midrise housing, and outer urban crown.

Polished persistent state after Bloom corrections: map, handoff, plan, and doctrine now prioritize source coordinate contracts, deterministic noise for organic occupancy, batched dense detail, domain-facing inspection renders, and verifier checks as reusable machine behavior rather than one-off scene scars.

Created the first reusable vibegeometry Python helper library for coordinate frames, deterministic fields, dense mesh batching, Blender scene primitives, and verifier helpers; refactored the Bloom build and verifier to use it, then rebuilt and verified the scene in Blender with 1028 geometry/text objects.

Built and verified the Lucent tether habitat from Ghostlight source: tether axis, city bubble below, media-eye transfer lounge, glass rail/safe-line threshold, Lucent overlay/control surfaces, and an inspectable VG Lucent Feed Ribbon Geometry Script group. Render review accepted overview, lounge, and city-bubble inspection artifacts.

Revised Lucent tether habitat after visual review: city bubble now has explicit tether load spine, elevator umbilical, collar, stays, and transfer node; renders now use Cycles; shader graphs add procedural noise/bump/ramp nodes to glass, tether, and Lucent overlay surfaces; verifier checks these contracts.

Replaced Lucent city-bubble grid with street-generation-inspired radial/tangential field roads, irregular annular parcel cells, anchor-plaza prestige skyscrapers, park voids, midcity blocks, and faux-rural cottagecore edge falloff; tether now visibly descends to the city ground-surface plaza.

Corrected Lucent dome city coordinate frame: ground is now a YZ disk orthogonal to the tether/spin axis, buildings extrude along +X, dome is boolean-cut flat at the base, city fills to the rim, and a Perlin-edged sunflower ray-floret crest rises from the base partway up the dome.

Moved Lucent sunflower ray florets outside the dome: alternating solar panel and radiator meshes now radiate sunward from the flat base rim, with verifier checks that their geometry remains outside the dome cut.

Scaled Lucent external ray florets to Citadel-like utility arms: alternating solar/radiator panels now dwarf the dome and span far along the tether, so a folded defensive posture can plausibly reach toward the media-eye end.

Added vg_csg, a Rust workspace crate for the new interactive Bevy-compatible CSG path: RealtimeCSG-inspired brush ordering and generations, exact AABB box subtraction, additive cylinder/dome/floret primitives, mesh-array output, DSL, tests, clippy, example, and research doctrine. Public RealtimeCSG source informed the shape but not the hidden native kernel.

Upgraded vg_csg from AABB-only subtraction to a convex-brush stepping-stone kernel: oriented-box brushes, plane splitting, convex fragment emission, angled-cut tests, and updated RealtimeCSG doctrine. Public LogicalError Realtime-CSG-demo and Sander van Rossen blog/article material are now preferred clean research inputs for the faster target model: polygon classification through boolean trees, not decompiled native internals.

Distilled public Realtime CSG ecosystem doctrine from Sander van Rossen blog posts, LogicalError/Realtime-CSG-demo, BSO3, GDC talk listing, and downstream engine discussions: target vg_csg should migrate from fragment carving toward polygon classification through boolean trees with explicit categories, half-edge/topology working data, bounds early-outs, dirty affected-brush rebuilds, separate mesh optimization, and CSG as one procedural grammar operator.

Built the first Dream Machine grammar layer: new vg_grammar crate with semantic solid/void ClaimTree, GrammarContext materials, RoomSpec/CorridorSpec/DoorSpec rules, a dream_room example, and tests proving grammar emits claims that compile into vg_csg brushes and leave doorway voids empty. The authoring split is now grammar writes spatial claims, CSG adjudicates matter, mesh is cache.

Extended the Dream Machine grammar with local frames, oriented claims, deterministic seeded variation, RuleSet composition, and GalleryChainSpec. The dream_room example now emits a rotated four-room chain as 42 semantic claims into vg_csg; workspace tests and clippy prove the grammar still preserves void claims through CSG assembly.

Added exact parity testing doctrine and first vg_csg parity fixtures for public RealtimeCSG-overlapping seams: polygon category vocabulary, six-plane/six-polygon box brush counts, and six surviving regions for centered box subtraction. Future matching API surface should get countable parity fixtures, not approximate plausibility checks.

Expanded the Rust API toward RealtimeCSG shape: added CsgTreeArena, CsgTree/CsgTreeBranch/CsgTreeBrush handles, CsgOperationType ordinals, CsgBranchOp vocabulary, branch child/operation mutation, brush operation mutation, tree-to-assembler compilation, grammar ClaimTree CSG tree emission, and parity fixtures for operation ordinals, branch vocabulary, and handle topology.

Advanced vg_csg toward the category-router kernel: ConvexPolygon now carries category/visibility/reversal/bounds metadata, primitives/solids/tree nodes expose bounds for early rejection, whole non-crossing polygons classify as inside/outside/aligned/reverse-aligned against convex cutters, convex intersection now powers Intersect brushes and Common branches, and parity/workspace tests keep the RealtimeCSG-shaped API seams honest.

Added CSG performance fixtures and advanced parity surface: vg_csg now has a release JSONL timing fixture with room-grid, rotated-cut, common-chain, distant-cutter, and centered-cut scenarios; tools/run_csg_perf.ps1 records our timings and an explicit missing reference slot; ConvexSolid can now split crossing polygons into inside/outside/aligned/reverse-aligned category buckets, matching the public demo shape before boolean routing.

Added a standalone RealtimeCSG C++ P/Invoke bridge that mirrors the managed Unity wrapper surface and fails closed for zero mesh descriptions. The native DLL loads and creates brush meshes, brushes, and trees, but outside Unity's hosted lifecycle GenerateMeshDescriptions currently returns success with count 0 even for an additive cube, so native timings are not yet valid.

RealtimeCSG native bridge now reaches mesh extraction and timing: matching the public Foundation path (brushes first, tree second, InsertChildNodeRange, no redundant default brush setters) converts the direct DLL bridge from zero mesh descriptions to health cube mesh descriptions and full perf fixture records.

CSG speed pass kept parity tests green while adding an axis-aligned box fast path, compiled brush geometry cache, owned convex splitting, clipping allocation reserves, and stable-generation output caching. Stable-tree fixture now beats or matches the direct C++ bridge on sampled scenarios, but dirty dense rotated subtraction still needs category-router work.

Added explicit stable and dirty vg_csg perf modes, public rebuild() bypassing stable output cache, cached-vs-dirty output parity test, and convex containment shortcuts. Latest fixture shows dirty box-heavy and distant-rejection paths are respectable; dirty dense rotated subtraction remains the next category-router target.

EpiphanyAquarium persisted memory changes the vg_csg acceleration prior: before adding a generic BVH/grid/sweep index, prototype demand-shaped spatial frontiers driven by dirty brushes, branch bounds, requested output scope, and visible/tile surfaces. GigaVoxels/froxel/Dreams doctrine treats rays or consumers as the query oracle; indexes are storage layouts for that frontier, not the central idea.

Started the meaner vg_csg pass: persisted the CSG roadmap, added exported DemandFrontier/DemandPair for ordered brush affected-pair planning, and added BuildReport/perf JSONL candidate_pairs and rejected_pairs counters along existing bounds gates. Tests/clippy pass; perf counters show rotated_cut_stack_64 still burns many rejected fragment/operator checks, so category-router plus frontier batching remains the next code target.

Added the first experimental vg_csg routed-surface path: single convex source plus exactly one candidate subtractive convex cutter emits surviving source boundary polygons plus reversed cutter cap polygons, reducing centered-cut triangles from 72 fragment-carver triangles to 48 boundary triangles. Dense repeated cutters are deliberately not routed yet: naive surface-list routing caused surface-piece explosion, so the next dense-kernel move must use compact category/frontier batching and scratch storage rather than unbounded polygon Vec churn.

Added PolygonRouteScratch for routed polygon classification and wired it into rebuild_routed_surfaces. An attempted in-place fragment frontier sped nothing safely: it changed rotated_cut_stack_64 output shape, so it was reverted and a regression now pins that scenario at 280 fragments, 3404 triangles, 804 candidate pairs, and 9417 rejected pairs. Future dense speed work must preserve ordered output or introduce an explicit accepted-output transition, not silently mutate semantics.

Added the first realtime-editing seam to vg_csg: brushes can now mutate primitive/op by BrushId, invalidating cached output and incrementing generation; DirtyDemandFrontier computes the conservative ordered suffix after the first dirty brush, identifying the future prefix-cache boundary. This is a planning/cache seam, not yet incremental mesh rebuild.

Added box-only dirty prefix checkpoints to vg_csg. build_incremental resumes from the checkpoint before the first dirty brush and parity tests compare incremental output against full rebuilds. Perf fixture now includes incremental_dirty; tail edits improve in large box-only scenarios, but final mesh emission remains whole-output and non-box paths fall back.

Extended vg_csg incremental dirty rebuilds from box-only state to the general convex builder. The cache now tracks a valid prefix boundary so tail edits reuse the checkpoint before the dirty brush without deep-copying stale checkpoint history. Latest smoke fixture shows large tail-edit wins, including rotated_cut_stack_64 dirty mean dropping from about 3.49 ms full rebuild to about 0.42 ms incremental, with parity tests still green.

Added conservative mesh reuse for vg_csg incremental dirty rebuilds. Mutations no longer discard the previous BuildOutput; suffix replay tracks whether geometry was touched, and rejected dirty suffixes reuse the prior mesh exactly. BuildReport exposes reused_mesh and tests cover distant tail edits. Candidate edits still emit the whole mesh.
